title: matelea scopulorum
intro: Matelea scopulorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane family. This plant is native to Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The leaves and stems of Matelea scopulorum contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to various health benefits. Herbal preparations of Matelea scopulorum are typically made by drying the leaves and stems, and infusing them in hot water to produce a tea that can be consumed to potentially alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
